content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings SSS and TTT. 

Find the length of the largest string KKK such that is subsequence of both SSS and TTT.


Input Format

First line of the input contains the string SSS.
Second line of the input contains the string TTT.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum size of the subsequence that is common in SSS and TTT.
constraints: S and T consist of lowercase English letters only
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line is the string S, the second line is the string T. Both strings contain only lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the length of the longest common subsequence of S and T.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The longest common subsequence between "code" and "chef" can be "ce" (or "co"), giving length 2.
The two strings share no common characters, so the LCS length is 0.
First pair: LCS length is 4 (e.g., "gtab"). Second pair: LCS length is 3 (e.g., "adh").

solution.approach: We use classic DP. Let dp[i][j] be the LCS length of the prefixes S[0..i-1] and T[0..j-1]. Transition: if S[i-1]==T[j-1] then dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1 else dp[i][j]=max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]). Initialise first row and column with 0. The answer for a test case is dp[|S|][|T|].
solution.spaceComplexity: O(|S| * |T|) integers (can be reduced to O(min(|S|,|T|)) but full table is used for clarity).
